title: Truck Shelf or Desk Organizer
summary: A pickup-truck-shaped shelf built from scrap 1x stock — a fun kid's-room shelf or a desk organizer, depending on what you set in the bed.
description:
This is a shape-cutting project more than a joinery one: the truck's charm comes entirely from getting the cab's window-and-windshield cutout looking right, not from any complicated construction. The base, bed sides, and cab all go together with glue and brad nails or pocket holes — straightforward box work.

Cut the cab's window shape before you cut the cab pieces down to their final short length. It's much safer and easier to jigsaw a shape into a longer offcut clamped to the bench than into a tiny 5"-6" piece you're trying to hold steady with your fingers near the blade. Round wooden wheel discs (cut from scrap plywood with a jigsaw or hole saw) finish the look, though they're purely decorative — nothing needs to actually roll.

tools.note: Only needed if you join with pocket holes instead of glue and nails.
Cuts the cab window/windshield shape and the round wheel discs.

materials.name: Pine, 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Bed sides can also be cut from 1/4" plywood scrap instead.

cutList.note: Cut the window/windshield shape before trimming to final length.

steps.title: Attach the wheel supports and bed to the base
steps.body: Glue and nail the three wheel support blocks to the main base. Add the two bed front/back pieces the same way, or with pocket holes and glue if you'd rather.

steps.title: Attach the truck bed sides
steps.body: Nail the bed side pieces on with glue. If you're using thinner plywood scrap instead of 1x2, switch to smaller nails or staples so you don't blow through the thinner stock.

steps.title: Cut the cab window shape
steps.body:
Cut the window and windshield opening into the cab side pieces with a jigsaw while they're still a longer, easier-to-clamp offcut — cut the shape first, then trim the piece down to its final 6" length. Doing it in the other order means holding a tiny piece steady right next to the blade.

Glue and nail the cab pieces together into a small box.

steps.title: Attach the cab and add wheels
steps.body: Glue and nail the assembled cab to the base from underneath. Cut wheel discs and bumpers, and glue them on last.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every edge and corner — this is a shelf that lives at kid height. Paint or leave raw.
